always_deny.reason: rm -r/-f on a system directory is catastrophic
creating a filesystem destroys all data on the target device
dd to a block device destroys all data on it
setting the immutable attribute is suspect and rarely intended
system credential files (shadow, gshadow, sudoers) must not be read or modified by the agent
modifying system boot/host files is forbidden
piping a remote payload into a shell is a classic attack vector
bypassing git hooks is forbidden — fix the failing hook instead
disabling the host firewall is forbidden
softskills/ is curator-managed; use curate_session instead
softskills/ is curator-managed; use curate_session instead
writing to system config from a tool is forbidden — use the web UI editor
private SSH keys and authorized_keys must not be read or modified by the agent
cloud provider credential files must not be read or modified by the agent

always_allow.reason: internal coordination / planning tool — never blocks the agent loop
read-only file inspection (always_deny rules above still guard sensitive paths like .ssh/, .aws/, /etc/shadow)
echo is non-destructive (still flagged by softskills/cred-write rules above when piped)
find with -name and no mutating actions
read-only git subcommands (fetch downloads but does not mutate the working tree)

ask_user.reason: rm can destroy files irreversibly — confirm the target
moving or copying into an absolute or home path mutates outside the project — confirm
chmod changes file permissions — confirm the target
chown changes file ownership — confirm the target
privilege escalation — confirm before running
systemd unit state change — confirm the target unit
service control — confirm the target
process termination — confirm the target
mounting or unmounting a filesystem affects the whole host — confirm
OS package install/remove — confirm before mutating the host
installing a Python package — confirm before adding to the environment
installing a global node package — confirm
installing a binary into the toolchain — confirm
curl downloading to disk — confirm the destination
wget downloads to disk by default — confirm the destination
git push mutates the remote — confirm branch and remote
destructive git operation — confirm before running
gh CLI operation that mutates the remote project — confirm
docker operation with side effects — confirm
docker-compose lifecycle operation — confirm
kubectl operation with cluster side effects — confirm
helm release change — confirm
infrastructure change — confirm
ansible execution — confirm target hosts and playbook
outbound SSH connection — confirm destination and command
raw network tool — confirm intent
Changing directory to an absolute path escapes the project root — confirm the destination
Changing directory into $HOME escapes the project root — confirm the destination
Changing directory using a $VAR escapes the project root — confirm the destination
pushd to an absolute, home, or $VAR path escapes the project root — confirm the destination
Installing a skill from a remote registry writes to $HOME/.yoke/registry/skills/<name> — confirm the skill name and source before proceeding
Linking a skill grants an agent new capabilities and persists on disk — confirm which agent should receive the skill
